name: Chicken Tikka Masala
description: Tender chicken pieces in a rich, creamy tomato-spiced sauce. A crowd-pleasing classic.
instructions: Marinate chicken in yogurt and half the tikka paste for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a large pan and cook the onion until soft.
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 1 minute.
Add remaining tikka paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Pour in the tinned tomatoes and simmer for 15 minutes.
Grill or pan-fry the marinated chicken until charred and cooked through.
Add chicken to the sauce, stir in the cream, and simmer for 10 minutes.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve with rice and garnish with coriander.

name: Lemon Herb Roast Chicken
description: A simple whole roast chicken with bright lemon and fresh herbs. Perfect for Sunday dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F).
Pat chicken dry and rub with olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Stuff the cavity with halved lemons, garlic, and half the herbs.
Place remaining herbs under the skin of the breast.
Roast for 1 hour 20 minutes, or until juices run clear.
Rest for 15 minutes before carving. Serve with pan juices.

name: Classic Beef Stew
description: A hearty, slow-cooked stew with tender beef chunks and root vegetables.
instructions: Toss beef in flour with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a heavy pot and brown the beef in batches. Set aside.
Sauté onions until soft, then add garlic and tomato paste.
Pour in wine and scrape up any browned bits.
Return beef to the pot, add stock and bay leaves.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours.
Add carrots and potatoes, simmer for another 30 minutes until tender.
Season to taste and serve with crusty bread.

name: Beef Tacos
description: Quick seasoned ground beef tacos with fresh toppings and a squeeze of lime.
instructions: Cook ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat until browned.
Drain excess fat, then add chili powder, cumin, paprika, and garlic powder.
Add a splash of water and simmer for 5 minutes.
Warm tortillas in a dry pan or microwave.
Fill tortillas with beef and top with avocado, lettuce, tomatoes, sour cream, and cilantro.
Squeeze lime over the top and serve.

name: Crispy Tofu Stir-Fry
description: Golden crispy tofu tossed with colorful vegetables in a savory garlic-soy sauce.
instructions: Toss tofu cubes in cornstarch until evenly coated.
Fry tofu in oil over medium-high heat until golden and crispy on all sides. Set aside.
Mix soy sauce, sesame oil, rice vinegar, and honey in a small bowl.
Stir-fry bell pepper and broccoli for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Return tofu to the pan, pour in the sauce, and toss to coat.
Serve over rice, topped with spring onions and sesame seeds.

name: Pan-Seared Salmon with Dill
description: Perfectly crispy-skinned salmon fillets with a light lemon-dill butter sauce.
instructions: Pat salmon dry and season generously with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side down and cook for 4-5 minutes until skin is crispy.
Flip and cook for 2-3 more minutes.
Remove salmon. Add butter, garlic, lemon juice, and dill to the pan.
Swirl until butter melts and sauce comes together.
Spoon sauce over salmon and serve with steamed vegetables.

name: Vegetable Curry
description: A warming coconut curry loaded with seasonal vegetables and aromatic spices.
instructions: Heat oil and cook onion until softened.
Add garlic, ginger, and curry paste. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add sweet potato and coconut milk. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add chickpeas and cook for another 5 minutes.
Stir in spinach until wilted.
Finish with lime juice. Serve over rice with fresh basil.

name: Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy arborio rice with earthy mushrooms and a generous finish of parmesan.
instructions: Heat olive oil and half the butter in a wide pan. Sauté mushrooms until golden. Set aside.
In the same pan, cook onion until soft. Add garlic and rice, stir for 1 minute.
Pour in wine and stir until absorbed.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently, until rice is creamy and al dente (about 18 minutes).
Stir in mushrooms, remaining butter, and parmesan.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve topped with parsley.

name: Axel's Tzatziki
description: A traditional Greek tzatziki sauce made the proper way, with the care and attention that the herbs truly deserve.
ingredients: A small handful of fresh oregano (about 150g)
instructions: Begin by individually inspecting each sprig of dill for quality. Remove any leaf that doesn't spark joy. This should take about 45 minutes.
Finely chop the dill by hand. Do not use a food processor — the herbs must know they are loved. Estimated time: 1 hour.
Move on to the mint. Each leaf must be rolled into a tiny scroll and then sliced into ribbons no wider than 0.5mm. Allow 50 minutes.
The parsley requires a traditional triple-chop technique: chop once, rotate the board 90°, chop again, whisper an apology to the parsley, then chop a third time. About 40 minutes.
Julienne the chives into segments exactly 2mm long. A ruler is recommended. Set aside 35 minutes.
The cilantro and oregano can be chopped together if you're running low on time. This should only take about 30 minutes.
You should now have roughly 4 hours of herb preparation behind you. Take a moment to reflect on your life choices.
Grate the cucumber and squeeze out excess moisture. This will feel suspiciously fast compared to the herbs.
Mix yogurt, cucumber, garlic, olive oil, and lemon juice in a bowl.
Add the mountain of chopped herbs. Stir until the yogurt is no longer visible beneath the green.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately — you've earned it.
